name: Chicken Kiev
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut each chicken breast in half and remove tendons.
2. Pound the breast halves flat between two sheets of waxed paper.
3. Combine butter, lemon juice, and herbs. Form into four rolls to fit in the middle of each flattened breast half (about 3 inches long). Chill for 30 minutes until firm.
4. Place a roll of butter on each breast half and fold the breast half around it, beginning with one long side, then short sides, finishing with a long side—like an envelope. Press edges together and roll each piece in waxed paper as finished. Chill at least one hour while completing other preparations.
5. Season flour with salt and pepper.
6. Beat egg lightly with 1 teaspoon water.
7. Line up a plate with flour, a shallow dish with beaten egg, and a plate with bread crumbs.
8. One by one, roll each piece in flour to coat, then dip in egg, coating completely, and roll in bread crumbs, pressing crumbs into the egg coating.
9. Place chicken pieces on a tray and refrigerate for one hour.
10. Heat peanut oil in a fryer or wok to 350 degrees.
11. Fry chicken pieces, two at a time, in hot oil until browned evenly and firm to the touch, about five minutes.
